violations.text: Beans were observed held below 135 degrees Fahrenheit on the shelf above the grill. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Beans were reheated at the time of inspection. Food shall not be held out of temperature control. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025
Beans were observed held past a day or date that exceeds 7 days. Beans were disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025

A knife was observed stored between prep units during use. PIC removed knife and brought it to 3-bay sink. Proper storing methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025
The door to the toilet room in the food storage area is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. At least one person must be certified in food safety at all times New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024

Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (cooked rice) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024

Desserts packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2024
The 3-bay sink was observed with a constant leak. The system shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2024
The door to the employee toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2024
